config_files.notes: Valorant uses 'Windows' rather than 'WindowsNoEditor' as the platform subdirectory — a UE4 shipping build convention. File is overwritten on every game exit. Edit only while Valorant is closed.
Engine-level overrides. Not overwritten by in-game settings changes.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Riot Vanguard (kernel-level anti-cheat) runs at all times when the Riot Games client is installed. It does not scan config files but does monitor for memory modifications. All keys in this profile are config-file based and Vanguard-safe.
engine_overrides.action: Safe to apply all recommended settings. Do not recommend third-party overlays that hook into game memory (e.g. some FPS unlockers). NVIDIA Overlay and Steam Overlay are safe.

engine_overrides.behaviour: Valorant's in-game Resolution Quality slider maps to sg.ResolutionQuality. However, setting values above 100 (super-sampling) via the .ini file does not persist — the game caps it at 100 on launch.
engine_overrides.action: Recommend values 50-100 only. Do not suggest super-sampling via this key.

keys.effect: Render resolution scale as a percentage. Range 50-100.
keys.notes: Valorant is CPU-limited on most hardware, not GPU-limited. Reducing resolution quality rarely provides meaningful FPS gains unless the GPU is genuinely the bottleneck. On low-end dedicated or integrated graphics, 75 is effective.
Low shadows improve both FPS and competitive visibility — shadows can obscure agent silhouettes in dark corners. Low is the professional player standard.
Valorant is not texture-heavy. High (2) is appropriate even on performance hardware — the VRAM cost is low and texture clarity aids enemy identification.
Low PostProcess removes bloom, lens flare, and ambient occlusion. Improves visual clarity and FPS consistency. Competitive standard is Low or Medium.
Low effects reduce particle density during ability-heavy fights, stabilising frame time in Sova recon drones, Brimstone smoke formations, and Raze explosive clusters.

engine_ini_keys.notes: Universally disabled in competitive play. Also improves motion comfort.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: MSAA 4x for quality. None for maximum FPS. MSAA produces cleaner edges than TAA at Valorant's map geometry style.
manual_only_settings.reason: AA method is set in-game only.

manual_only_settings.location: Settings > Video > NVIDIA Reflex Low Latency
manual_only_settings.recommendation: Enabled + Boost on all NVIDIA GPUs. Reduces click-to-screen latency by 10-30ms in GPU-bound scenarios.
manual_only_settings.reason: Reflex is in-game toggle only. No effect on AMD/Intel hardware.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Fullscreen (exclusive) for lowest input latency. Use Windowed Fullscreen only if alt-tabbing frequently.
manual_only_settings.reason: Display mode selection stored in GameUserSettings.ini but best set in-game to avoid resolution conflicts.

manual_only_settings.location: Settings > Video > Limit FPS in Menus
manual_only_settings.recommendation: 30 FPS. Valorant's main menu and collection screens are GPU-intensive. Capping menu FPS prevents the GPU from running at full load while idle.

notes: Valorant is predominantly CPU-limited, not GPU-limited, on most hardware tiers. FPS gains from GPU settings are smaller than in most other titles — CPU frequency, background process count, and RAM speed have more impact.
The config path uses 'Windows' not 'WindowsNoEditor' — a common source of confusion when copying UE4 guides from other games.
Vanguard requires Secure Boot and TPM 2.0 on Windows 11. If Valorant fails to launch, the issue is typically Vanguard anti-cheat hardware requirements rather than a config problem.
Valorant regularly pushes graphics setting resets with major patches. Users should check GameUserSettings.ini after major client updates.
